description:
Your Zotero library contains 25 candidate papers from a literature search on 'deep learning in computational neuroscience.' Prepare the library for use in a systematic review.

Merge any duplicate entries, keeping the copy with more complete metadata. Move all papers published before 2000 to the Trash. Papers whose abstract reads 'Abstract not available' are incomplete — tag each with 'incomplete-record' and add them to a new collection called 'Needs Manual Review.' Standardize abbreviated venue names: use 'Current Opinion in Neurobiology' (not 'Curr. Opin. Neurobiol.') and 'Annual Review of Vision Science' (not 'Annu. Rev. Vis. Sci.'). Finally, create a collection called 'Included Studies' containing all remaining screened-in papers, add a standalone note titled 'Screening Summary' to it, and export the collection as BibTeX to /home/ga/Desktop/included_studies.bib.

pre2000_titles: A Quantitative Description of Membrane Current and its Application to Conduction and Excitation in Nerve
Neural Networks and Physical Systems with Emergent Collective Computational Abilities
Receptive Fields, Binocular Interaction and Functional Architecture in the Cat's Visual Cortex
incomplete_abstract_titles: Performance-optimized hierarchical models predict neural responses in higher visual cortex
Vector-based navigation using grid-like representations in artificial agents
A Task-Optimized Neural Network Replicates Human Auditory Behavior, Predicts Brain Responses, and Reveals a Cortical Processing Hierarchy
If deep learning is the answer, what is the question?
duplicate_titles: A deep learning framework for neuroscience
Toward an Integration of Deep Learning and Neuroscience
